$comment: Canonical source of the /etc/umbra/images.json contract. Lives in UmbraOS at schema/images-schema.json and is vendored verbatim into Umbra Studio at crates/umbra-core/schema/images-schema.json. Both copies are byte-identical; edit this file upstream in UmbraOS only and re-vendor. Both sides validate against it in CI.
title: Umbra lab image catalog

schema.description: Catalog schema version. Only 1 is understood.

sri_sha256.description: Subresource-Integrity-style SHA-256, as Nix emits it: 'sha256-' followed by standard base64 of the 32-byte digest (43 base64 chars + one '=' pad).

sha256.description: Hash of the artifact at its source. For bundled: the store disk. For provisioned: the file at 'url' as downloaded — i.e. the archive itself when 'archive' is set, not the unwrapped disk.

format.description: On-disk container format of the (unwrapped) disk.

store_path.description: Bundled only. Absolute /nix/store path of the disk.

url.description: Provisioned only. Vendor URL the user fetches from.

install_path.description: Provisioned only. Where the fetched disk is placed.

license_note.description: Provisioned only. Redistribution/licensing note shown before fetch.

archive.description: Provisioned only. Present iff 'url' is a wrapped archive that must be extracted to obtain the disk.

extracted_sha256.description: Provisioned+archived only. Hash of the disk AFTER extraction, which the supervisor actually boots. Required iff 'archive' is set.

bundled.$comment: Ships in the read-only Nix store; always present, nothing to fetch.

provisioned.$comment: Non-redistributable; fetched on user consent into install_path.

oneOf.$comment: Direct: 'url' is the bare disk. Verify download, then rename.
Archived: 'url' is wrapped. Verify archive (sha256), extract, verify disk (extracted_sha256), rename.
